question.content: AT and BC intersect at point E. Find the ratio BE:CE. Answer format requirement: <expr>. Strictly forbidden: any extra text beyond this format.

step_1.content: Start from E = AT ∩ BC and treat the goal as deriving BE:CE from angle-linked similar triangles on BC.

step_2.content: Use the right-side construction through A-D-K to set up angle correspondences linked back to line BC.
step_2.key_point: The ADK branch provides the angle bridge from A,T-side geometry to B,C-side geometry.

step_4.content: Apply cyclic-angle relations from the two auxiliary circles to form one pair of similar triangles containing BE and CE as corresponding sides.
step_4.key_point: Circle-based equal angles are the core reason the segment ratio on BC becomes constrained.

step_6.content: From the similarity result, obtain BE/CE = 1 directly, so E divides BC internally in the ratio 1:1.
step_6.key_point: Therefore the required ratio is exactly 1.
